---
title: "NSCLCORIEN Differential Microbial Abundance"
author: "Dipankor Dhrubo"
date: "2025-09-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Loading required packages
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
library(ggsurvfit)
library(survminer)
library(ggrepel)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(DESeq2)
library(igraph)
library(purrr)
library(rlang)
library(mt.surv)
```

```{r}
# Read the file
processed<- readRDS("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/NSCLC_metadata.rds") %>% 
  select(sample,Candida ,CAlevel, status, diagsurvtime) %>% unique()

Abundance<- read_csv("/fs/ess/PAS1695/projects/exotho/data/THO_decontaminated-counts_taxonomy_2025-05-05.csv.gz") %>% select(-1)
```

```{r}
# Summing microbial counts for each genus within each sample
df_summed <- Abundance %>%
  group_by(sample, genus) %>%
  summarise(Total_Count = sum(counts, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

df_summed<- df_summed %>% filter(!genus == "g__Candida")

# Transform to wide format for easier analysis
df_wide <- df_summed %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = genus, values_from = Total_Count, values_fill = 0)

# View transformed data
head(df_wide)


# Calculate non-zero counts per genus
non_zero_counts <- colSums(df_wide[-1] > 0)

# Calculate percentage of samples with non-zero counts
non_zero_percentage <- non_zero_counts / nrow(df_wide) * 100

# Inspect the distribution of non-zero percentages
hist(non_zero_percentage, breaks = 50, main = "Non-Zero Percentages of Microbes", xlab = "Percentage")

# Set a threshold (e.g., genera present in at least 10% of samples)
min_samples <- nrow(df_wide) * 0.10

# Filter out low-prevalence genera
filtered_df <- df_wide %>%
  select(sample, where(~ sum(. > 0) >= min_samples))

# Check the new dimensions after filtering
dim(filtered_df)


```

```{r}
### Preparatory work DESeq2

## Making sure we are only working with NSCLC patients

filtered_df_2<- filtered_df[filtered_df$sample %in% processed$sample,] %>% 
  left_join(processed %>% select(sample,CAlevel,Candida),by = "sample") 

clinical<- filtered_df_2 %>% select(sample, CAlevel)

filtered_df_2<- filtered_df_2[, -c(163,164)] #  Forgot what I removed here. But nothing important


# Transpose the data and keep the first column as row names
transposed_df <- as.data.frame(t(filtered_df_2[-1]))  # Exclude sample column during transpose

# Rename columns with sample names
colnames(transposed_df) <- filtered_df_2$sample
clinical<- clinical %>% column_to_rownames("sample")

# Check the transposed data
head(transposed_df)

transposed_df<- transposed_df %>% select(rownames(clinical))

all(colnames(transposed_df) == rownames(clinical)) ## Make sure this holds true.

```

```{r}
## Differential microbial abundance

# Prepare DESeq2 dataset
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = transposed_df,
                              colData = clinical,
                              design = ~ CAlevel)

# Run DESeq2 analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)
res <- results(dds)
```

```{r}

## Processing the deseq2 output file

# View top differentially abundant genera
res_sorted <- res[order(res$pvalue),]

res_sorted<- as.data.frame(res_sorted)

res_sorted$Abundance<- "NO"
res_sorted$Abundance[res_sorted$log2FoldChange >= 0.8 & res_sorted$padj < 0.05] <- "UP"
res_sorted$Abundance[res_sorted$log2FoldChange <= -0.8 & res_sorted$padj < 0.05] <- "Down"

res_sorted <- tibble::rownames_to_column(res_sorted, var = "Genus")
```

```{r}
### These codes are for labling purposes. You can ignore these
top_up <- res_sorted %>%
  filter(log2FoldChange > 1, padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  head(10)

top_down <- res_sorted %>%
  filter(log2FoldChange < -1, padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  head(10)

# Combine the top genera for labeling
top_genera <- rbind(top_up, top_down)

res_sorted$delabel <- ifelse(res_sorted$log2FoldChange %in% top_genera$log2FoldChange, as.character(res_sorted$Genus), NA)
```

```{r}

# Volcano plot

ggplot(data = res_sorted, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), col = Abundance, label = delabel)) +
  
  # Add vertical and horizontal threshold lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') + 
  
  # Scatter plot of points with custom colors
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  
  # Custom color scheme for differential expression categories
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#00AFBB", "grey", "#bb0c00"), 
                     labels = c("Downregulated", "Not significant", "Upregulated")) +
  
  # Limit the x and y axes to avoid infinite values
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 150), xlim = c(-4, 4)) + 
  
  # Add custom labels and title
  labs(color = 'Abundance',
       x = expression("log"[2]*"FC"),
       y = expression("-log"[10]*"p-value"),
       title = "Volcano Plot (Low Candida vs. High Candida)") +
  
  # Customize x-axis breaks
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-10, 10, 2)) +
  
  # Add text labels using geom_text_repel to prevent overlap
  geom_text_repel(data = top_genera, aes(label = Genus), 
                  size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf, box.padding = 0.5, 
                  point.padding = 0.3, segment.size = 0.2, color = "black") +
  
  # Apply a clean theme
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold",size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold",size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold",size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold",size = 14)
        )


ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLCORIEN_Differential_microbes_lowCAvshighCA.png", width = 8, height = 6)
write_rds(res_sorted,"/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Data/NSCLCORIEN_Differential_microbes_result.rda")

```



```{r}


# Select up-regulated and down-regulated microbes
top_up <- res_sorted %>%
  filter(Abundance == "UP")

top_down <- res_sorted %>%
  filter(Abundance == "Down")

# Combine significant microbes
top_microbes <- rbind(top_up, top_down)

# Preparing file for only significant microbes

top_genera<- top_microbes$Genus


Up_down_microbe <- df_wide %>%
  select(sample, all_of(top_genera))


Final_microbe_surv<- Up_down_microbe %>% left_join(processed %>% 
                                                     select(sample,Candida),
                                                   by="sample") %>% dplyr::rename(g__Candida = Candida) %>% unique()
 
samples<- Final_microbe_surv$sample
Final_microbe_surv<- Final_microbe_surv[,-1]



#Co-abundance network creation

## Preparing data. Taking only the count the for significant microbes

abundance_matrix <- as.data.frame(Final_microbe_surv)
rownames(abundance_matrix) <- samples

# ensure numeric
abundance_matrix[] <- lapply(abundance_matrix, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

# 1) Normalize to relative abundance ----------------------------------
abundance_rel <- sweep(abundance_matrix, 1, rowSums(abundance_matrix), FUN = "/")
abundance_rel[is.na(abundance_rel)] <- 0  # rows that summed to 0

# OPTIONAL: prevalence filter to reduce ties/noise (e.g., present in ≥ 5% samples)
prev <- colMeans(abundance_rel > 0)
keep <- prev >= 0.05
abundance_rel <- abundance_rel[, keep, drop = FALSE]

# 2) Build all pairs ---------------------------------------------------
microbes <- colnames(abundance_rel)
all_pairs <- combn(microbes, 2)

# 3) Spearman for each pair (asymptotic p-values to avoid tie warnings) ----
cor_results <- apply(all_pairs, 2, function(pair) {
  x <- abundance_rel[, pair[1]]
  y <- abundance_rel[, pair[2]]
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y,
                                  method = "spearman",
                                  use = "pairwise.complete.obs",
                                  exact = FALSE))
  c(source = pair[1], target = pair[2],
    rho = unname(ct$estimate), pval = ct$p.value,
    n = sum(stats::complete.cases(x, y)))
})

# Step 3: Convert to data frame
cor_df <- as.data.frame(t(cor_results), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
cor_df <- cor_df %>%
  mutate(rho  = as.numeric(rho),
         pval = as.numeric(pval),
         n    = as.numeric(n),
         padj = p.adjust(pval, method = "BH")) %>%
  arrange(padj, desc(abs(rho)))

head(cor_df)

# Step 5: Optional filtering (e.g., |rho| ≥ 0.5 & padj < 0.05)
cor_filtered <- cor_df %>%
  filter(abs(rho) >= 0.2, padj < 0.05)

# View top hits
head(cor_filtered)

cor_filtered_2 <- cor_df %>%
  filter(abs(rho) >= 0.5, padj < 0.05)


## This plot will give the idea which rho should be used as threshold for next analysis

ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = rho, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.5, 0.5), linetype = "dashed", color = "green") +
  labs(x = "Spearman ρ", y = "-log10(adjusted p-value)",
       title = "adjP vs Rho") + ylim(-100, 500)+
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/exotho/exploratory/Figures/
       ORIEN_NSCLC2025/Pval_vs_rho.png", height = 10, width = 8)


# 0.5 is considered as a threshold to reduce noise

cor_threshold <- 0.4
# choose which table you want (be consistent!)
M <- abundance_matrix   # or abundance_rel

# columns = microbes
cor_matrix <- cor(abundance_rel, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")


# Use the absolute value of correlations to avoid negative weights
adjacency_matrix <- abs(cor_matrix) * (abs(cor_matrix) >= cor_threshold)

# Remove self-correlations (diagonal)
diag(adjacency_matrix) <- 0

# Convert to an edge list
cor_edges <- as.data.frame(as.table(adjacency_matrix))
cor_edges <- cor_edges %>%
  filter(Freq > cor_threshold) %>%
  dplyr::rename(source = Var1, target = Var2, weight = Freq) %>%
  distinct(source, target, .keep_all = TRUE)  # Remove duplicates

# Check the resulting edge list
head(cor_edges)

# Load required packages
library(igraph)

network <- graph_from_data_frame(d = cor_edges, directed = FALSE)

# Add node attributes (degree centrality)
V(network)$degree <- degree(network)

# Check the network structure
print(network)


# Generate a simplified network plot
set.seed(42)  # For reproducibility

# Simplify the network by removing multiple and loop edges
simple_network <- simplify(network, remove.multiple = TRUE, remove.loops = TRUE)

# Calculate degree centrality for node size
V(simple_network)$size <- degree(simple_network)

# Set colors: red for Candida, blue for other microbes
V(simple_network)$color <- ifelse(V(simple_network)$name == "g__Candida", "red", "skyblue")

# Highlight hubs (nodes with high degree)
hub_threshold <- mean(degree(simple_network)) + sd(degree(simple_network))
V(simple_network)$label <- ifelse(V(simple_network)$size > hub_threshold | V(simple_network)$name == "g__Candida", V(simple_network)$name, NA)

#######################

####################

# Plot the microbial co-abundance network with all microbe names
# Plot the microbial co-abundance network with labels slightly moved upwards
# Plot the microbial co-abundance network with closer components


library(Cairo)

CairoPNG("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLCORIEN_Candida_network.png",
         width = 10, height = 12, units = "in", dpi = 300)

set.seed(1)
lay <- layout_with_fr(simple_network)
plot(simple_network, layout = lay, vertex.size = 5,
     vertex.color = V(simple_network)$color,
     vertex.label = V(simple_network)$name,
     vertex.label.cex = 0.8, vertex.label.color = "black",
     vertex.label.dist = 1.5, vertex.label.degree = -pi/2,
     edge.color = "gray", edge.width = 0.5,
     main = "Co-Abundance Network (|ρ| ≥ 0.4, padj < 0.05)")
dev.off()


```


```{r}
######### For Non candida module (module where candida is absent)

# Get Candida's module ID
modules <- cluster_louvain(simple_network)

# Assign microbe names to the membership vector
names(modules$membership) <- V(simple_network)$name
modules$membership

names(modules$membership)
# Get the module of Candida
candida_module_id <- modules$membership["g__Candida"]

# Get Module 2 (non-Candida)
non_candida_module_id <- setdiff(unique(modules$membership), candida_module_id)

# Microbes in Module 2
non_candida_module_members <- names(modules$membership)[modules$membership %in% non_candida_module_id]

module_score_df_2 <- abundance_rel %>%
  select(all_of(non_candida_module_members)) %>%
  mutate(module_score = rowSums(across(everything()), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("sample")

Final_microbe_surv<- Up_down_microbe %>% left_join(processed %>% 
                                                     select(sample,Candida, diagsurvtime, status,CAlevel),
                                                   by="sample") %>% dplyr::rename(g__Candida = Candida) %>% unique()

merged_df_2 <- Final_microbe_surv %>%
  select(sample, diagsurvtime, status,CAlevel) %>%
  left_join(module_score_df_2, by = "sample")



# Model: Module score + Candida level
cox_module <- coxph(Surv(diagsurvtime, status) ~ module_score + CAlevel, data = merged_df_2)


```


```{r}

## Forest plot 

coxresults <- broom::tidy(cox_module, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)
summary(cox_module)


coxresults <- coxresults %>%
  mutate(term = forcats::fct_rev(term)) ## Making things in reverse order

coxresults <- coxresults %>%
  mutate(pval_label = sprintf("p = %.3f", p.value)) %>%
  mutate(term = recode(term,
                       "module_score"  = "Module Score",
                       "CAlevelHigh" = "Candida (High vs Low, ref=Low)"))


# Calculate a constant x-position to place all p-values
x_position <- max(coxresults$conf.high, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.5

# Create the forest plot
ggplot(coxresults[-56,], aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(aes(x = x_position, label = pval_label), hjust = -.25, size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_log10(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.6))) +
  labs(x = "Hazard Ratio (95% CI)", y = "Predictor", title = "ORIEN Microbial Abundance") +
  theme_bw()+theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face="bold", size=12),
                   axis.text.y = element_text(face="bold", size=12),
                   axis.title=element_text(size=14,face="bold"))
ggsave("/users/PAS1695/dipankor99/Github/digital-twins/exploratory/Figures/NSCLCORIEN_Forestplot_Candida_vs_all.png", height = 5, width = 7)

```


```{r}
## Trying something new

df <- merged_df_2 %>%
  mutate(
    CAlevel = factor(CAlevel, levels = c("Low","High")),
    module_z = as.numeric(scale(module_me1 %||% module_score))  # z-score the module
  ) %>%
  select(diagsurvtime, status, CAlevel, module_z) %>%
  na.omit()

SurvObj <- with(df, Surv(diagsurvtime, status))

## Fit the three nested models

# M0: CA only
m0 <- coxph(SurvObj ~ CAlevel, data = df)

# M1: non-Candida module only
m1 <- coxph(SurvObj ~ module_z, data = df)

# M2: CA + non-Candida module
m2 <- coxph(SurvObj ~ CAlevel + module_z, data = df)

##Likelihood-ratio tests (nested comparisons)

# Does adding CA to the module improve fit? compare M1 vs M2

# Does adding the module to CA improve fit? compare M0 vs M2

lrt_CA_adds_value      <- anova(m1, m2, test = "LRT")  # CA on top of module
lrt_module_adds_value  <- anova(m0, m2, test = "LRT")  # module on top of CA

lrt_CA_adds_value
lrt_module_adds_value

# Harrell’s C-index and ΔC: It’s the survival-version of AUC. It asks: for two patients, does the model give a higher risk to the one who dies first?
# Range: 0.5 = no better than chance; 1.0 = perfect ranking.

##ΔC (delta C-index) : The change in C-index when you add (or remove) a predictor.

# Example: C goes from 0.502 → 0.515 when adding Candida ⇒ ΔC = +0.013.

# If ΔC > 0, the added variable improves discrimination (how well the model ranks risk). If ~0, it adds little.

get_c <- function(fit) unname(summary(fit)$concordance[1])

c0 <- get_c(m0)  # CA only
c1 <- get_c(m1)  # module only
c2 <- get_c(m2)  # CA + module

deltaC_CA_over_module     <- c2 - c1
deltaC_module_over_CA     <- c2 - c0

tibble(
  model = c("M0: CA only","M1: module only","M2: CA + module"),
  C_index = c(c0, c1, c2),
  deltaC_vs_prev = c(NA, c1 - c0, c2 - c1)
)
### Bootstrap C-index ΔC with CIs

set.seed(1)
B <- 500
boot_dc_CA  <- numeric(B)
boot_dc_mod <- numeric(B)

n <- nrow(df)
for (b in 1:B) {
  idx <- sample(seq_len(n), replace = TRUE)
  dB  <- df[idx, ]
  S   <- with(dB, Surv(diagsurvtime, status))
  m0B <- coxph(S ~ CAlevel, data = dB)
  m1B <- coxph(S ~ module_z, data = dB)
  m2B <- coxph(S ~ CAlevel + module_z, data = dB)
  c0B <- get_c(m0B); c1B <- get_c(m1B); c2B <- get_c(m2B)
  boot_dc_CA[b]  <- c2B - c1B
  boot_dc_mod[b] <- c2B - c0B
}
quantile(boot_dc_CA,  probs = c(.025,.5,.975))    # ΔC when adding CA
quantile(boot_dc_mod, probs = c(.025,.5,.975))    # ΔC when adding module


## Interpretation: Adding Candida to the module increased C-index by ~0.01 (95% CI −0.006 to 0.033); adding the module to Candida changed C by ~0.005 (95% CI −0.004 to 0.019). Likelihood-ratio tests favored models including Candida (p≈0.02). Thus, Candida carries the prognostic signal, while the non-Candida module adds minimal discrimination.

```
